description: Human-authored ground truth for Experiment 001: Knowledge Object extraction.
scope: Small benchmark for validating whether an LLM can extract meaningful Knowledge Objects from short STEM learning materials.
notes: This ground truth is intentionally small and hand-authored.
Object types are provisional for Experiment 001 and should not be treated as the final ADR-003 schema.
source_span is used for grounding individual Knowledge Objects. Evidence remains a Connection-layer concept.

expected_objects.name: Partial Derivative
expected_objects.source_span: A partial derivative measures how \(f\) changes when one coordinate changes while the other coordinates are held fixed.

expected_objects.name: Gradient
expected_objects.source_span: The gradient \(\nabla f(x)\) collects these partial derivatives into a vector.

expected_objects.name: Directional Derivative
expected_objects.source_span: For a unit direction \(u\), the directional derivative is \(\nabla f(x)\cdot u\), so the gradient points in the direction of steepest local increase.

expected_objects.name: Stationary Point
expected_objects.source_span: A point where the gradient is the zero vector is called a stationary point.

expected_objects.name: Taylor Approximation
expected_objects.source_span: Taylor approximation describes the local behavior of a differentiable function near a point.

expected_objects.name: First-order Taylor Approximation Formula
expected_objects.source_span: f(a+h) \approx f(a) + \nabla f(a)\cdot h.

expected_objects.name: Hessian Matrix
expected_objects.source_span: For twice differentiable functions, a second-order approximation also includes the Hessian matrix, which describes local curvature.

expected_objects.name: Vector Space
expected_objects.source_span: A vector space is a set of objects called vectors that can be added together and multiplied by scalars while satisfying the vector space axioms.

expected_objects.name: Basis
expected_objects.source_span: A basis is a set of independent vectors that can represent every vector in the space through linear combinations.

expected_objects.name: Dimension
expected_objects.source_span: The number of vectors in a basis is the dimension of the vector space.

expected_objects.name: Matrix
expected_objects.source_span: A matrix is a rectangular array of numbers that can represent a linear transformation once bases have been chosen for the input and output spaces.

expected_objects.name: Linear Transformation
expected_objects.source_span: Matrix multiplication corresponds to composing linear transformations, and changing a basis changes the matrix representation without changing the underlying transformation.

expected_objects.name: Eigenvector
expected_objects.source_span: For a square matrix \(A\), an eigenvector is a nonzero vector \(v\) whose direction is preserved by the transformation represented by \(A\).

expected_objects.name: Eigenvalue
expected_objects.source_span: The corresponding eigenvalue \(\lambda\) satisfies

expected_objects.name: Eigenvalue Equation
expected_objects.source_span: Av = \lambda v.

expected_objects.name: Characteristic Polynomial
expected_objects.source_span: Eigenvalues are connected to the characteristic polynomial \(\det(\lambda I - A)\).

expected_objects.name: Objective Function
expected_objects.source_span: In mathematical optimisation, an objective function assigns a real value to each feasible input.

expected_objects.name: Optimisation Problem
expected_objects.source_span: An optimisation problem asks for an input that makes the objective function as small or as large as possible.

expected_objects.name: Unconstrained Minimisation Problem
expected_objects.source_span: In an unconstrained minimisation problem, the feasible inputs are usually all points in a space such as \(\mathbb{R}^n\), and the goal is to minimise a differentiable function \(f(x)\).

expected_objects.name: Descent Direction
expected_objects.source_span: The negative gradient \(-\nabla f(x)\) is a descent direction because it points toward the direction of steepest local decrease.

expected_objects.name: Gradient Descent
expected_objects.source_span: Gradient descent is a first-order iterative method that repeatedly moves in this direction:

expected_objects.name: Gradient Descent Update
expected_objects.source_span: x_{k+1} = x_k - \alpha_k \nabla f(x_k).

expected_objects.name: Step Size
expected_objects.source_span: The scalar \(\alpha_k\) is the step size.

expected_objects.name: Line Search
expected_objects.source_span: A line search is a method for choosing a step size that gives sufficient decrease in the objective function.

expected_objects.name: Convex Function
expected_objects.source_span: For convex functions, every local minimum is also a global minimum.

expected_objects.name: Stationary Point
expected_objects.source_span: A stationary point is a point where the gradient is zero.
